metadata.title: Reflection paper on regulatory requirements for the development of medicinal products for non-alcoholic steatohepatitis (NASH)

rules.rule_text: Applicants are advised to seek scientific advice before translating the statements of this paper to other chronic liver diseases.
rules.verbatim_quote: Potential applicants are advised to seek scientific advice before translating the statements of this paper to other chronic liver diseases.

rules.rule_text: This document should be read in conjunction with the introduction and general principles and part I and II of the Annex I to Directive 2001/83/EC as amended.
rules.verbatim_quote: This document should be read in conjunction with the introduction and general principles and part I and II of the Annex I to Directive 2001/83/EC as amended.

rules.rule_text: Applicants intending a development in completely non-invasively diagnosed patient populations are recommended to apply for Scientific Advice.
rules.verbatim_quote: Applicants intending such a development are recommended to apply for Scientific Advice.
exceptions: Developments for standard biopsy-diagnosed patient populations are within scope of this reflection paper

rules.rule_text: An acceptable regulatory strategy for applicants developing new agents in the disease area may be to define intermediate endpoints for which a reasonable assumption for the prediction of long-term outcomes can be made.
rules.verbatim_quote: An acceptable regulatory strategy for applicants developing new agents in the disease area may be to define intermediate endpoints for which a reasonable assumption for the prediction of long-term outcomes can be made.

rules.rule_text: A conditional marketing authorisation strategy is only acceptable if an unmet need is still present, a positive benefit-risk ratio can be concluded, and it is likely that the applicant will be in a position to provide comprehensive clinical data post-marketing.
rules.verbatim_quote: Such a strategy, however, is only acceptable if an unmet need is still present, a positive benefit-risk ratio can be concluded, and it is likely that the applicant will be in a position to provide comprehensive clinical data post-marketing.

rules.rule_text: The acceptance of the conditional marketing authorisation regulatory strategy will be evaluated on a case-by-case basis.
rules.verbatim_quote: The acceptance of the mentioned regulatory strategy for CMA will be evaluated on a case-by-case basis.

rules.rule_text: Drug developers are encouraged to aim at producing evidence for future validation of novel methodologies intended to replace histology in the future within their drug development programs.
rules.verbatim_quote: Drug developers are therefore encouraged to aim at producing evidence for future validation of novel methodologies intended to replace histology in the future within their drug development programs.

rules.rule_text: At the time of inclusion (randomisation), patients should be requested to have stable weight for a certain timeframe.
rules.verbatim_quote: At the time of inclusion (=randomisation), patients should, however, rather be requested to have stable weight for a certain timeframe.

rules.rule_text: Co-morbidities such as diabetes, dyslipidaemia, and hypertension should be treated adequately and stably at the time of inclusion.
rules.verbatim_quote: Co-morbidities, such as diabetes, dyslipidaemia, and hypertension should be treated adequately and stably at the time of inclusion.

rules.rule_text: Clear instructions for handling of the treatment of co-morbidities need to be defined in the protocol.
rules.verbatim_quote: Clear instructions for handling of the treatment of co-morbidities need to be defined in the protocol.

rules.rule_text: Other chronic liver diseases should be excluded from study populations. The exclusion of relevant alcohol intake is required for the inclusion of patients into clinical studies and should be based on validated questionnaires, lifetime history of alcohol intake, as well as relevant biomarkers.
rules.verbatim_quote: Other chronic liver disease (such as viral hepatitis, PBC, PSC, autoimmune hepatitis, Wilson's disease, etc.) should be excluded. In addition, the exclusion of relevant alcohol intake is, according to the definition of the disease, required for the inclusion of patients into clinical studies. This should be based on validated questionnaires, lifetime history of alcohol intake, as well as relevant biomarkers.

rules.rule_text: Monitoring of alcohol intake (e.g., biomarker based) is recommended during clinical studies.
rules.verbatim_quote: Monitoring of alcohol intake (e.g., biomarker based) is recommended during clinical studies.

rules.rule_text: Histology should always be available, also in early clinical studies, and inclusion of patients should generally be based on histological evaluation (grading and staging).
rules.verbatim_quote: there is currently a broad consensus (e.g., see the multi-stakeholder composed Liver Forum publications) that histology should always be available, also in early clinical studies, and inclusion of patients should generally be based on histological evaluation (grading and staging).
exceptions: Deviations for exploratory clinical studies are possible if based on sound scientific principles, for which the uncertainties can be quantified, and later stage studies be planned accordingly

rules.rule_text: Biopsies should generally not be older than 6 months at the time of inclusion into a clinical study.
rules.verbatim_quote: Biopsies should generally not be older than 6 months at the time of inclusion into a clinical study (for potential exceptions, see below).
exceptions: Potential exceptions exist as described in the document

rules.rule_text: The NASH-clinical research network (CRN) grading system is the recommended grading system for histological diagnosis for inclusion and evaluation of histology endpoints.
rules.verbatim_quote: For the histological diagnosis for inclusion (and evaluation of histology endpoints) the NASH-clinical research network (CRN) grading system is the recommended grading system.
exceptions: Patients may also be included and evaluated based on other grading systems for NASH (e.g. SAF score), provided the validation of respective grading systems is substantiated

rules.rule_text: For the conventional evaluation of histology, in order to limit variability of the methods, use of centralised evaluation by at least two experienced histopathologists, including algorithms for arbitration is recommended.
rules.verbatim_quote: For the "conventional" evaluation of histology, in order to limit variability of the methods, use of centralised evaluation by at least two experienced histopathologists, including algorithms for arbitration is recommended.

rules.rule_text: Applicants wishing to use alternative histological evaluation methodologies (e.g., artificial intelligence-aided methods) are advised to seek scientific advice.
rules.verbatim_quote: since alternative methods have not been fully validated yet, applicants wishing to use different methodologies are advised to seek scientific advice.

rules.rule_text: For non-cirrhotic NASH studies, if the NAS-CRN system is used, a total NAS of greater or equal to 4 with at least a score of 1 for ballooning and lobular inflammation each should be used.
rules.verbatim_quote: If the NAS-CRN system is used, a total NAS of greater or equal than 4 with at least a score of 1 for ballooning and lobular inflammation each should be used.

rules.rule_text: Inclusion of patients based on non-invasive methods excluding biopsy/histology based on clinical features, imaging, and/or biomarkers in confirmatory studies is generally not recommended.
rules.verbatim_quote: Currently, inclusion of patients based on non-invasive methods excluding biopsy/histology based on clinical features, imaging, and/or biomarkers in confirmatory studies is generally not recommended.

rules.rule_text: The use of clinical features and non-invasive methods to identify a high proportion of patients fulfilling the histology criteria during screening are recommended to avoid unnecessary biopsies.
rules.verbatim_quote: However, the use of clinical features and non-invasive methods to identify a high proportion of patients fulfilling the histology criteria during screening are recommended to avoid unnecessary biopsies.

rules.rule_text: In patients with compensated cirrhotic NASH where definite NASH is not present, all of the following features should be documented: historical biopsies with presence of unequivocal NASH, a high likelihood of NASH based on non-invasive testing, and the presence of associated co-morbidity.
rules.verbatim_quote: in case definite NASH is not present, all of the following features should be documented in order to make the diagnosis NASH sufficiently likely: Historical biopsies with presence of unequivocal NASH, a high likelihood of NASH based on non-invasive testing (biomarker and imaging; criteria need to be clearly defined), and the presence of associated co-morbidity (e.g. at least two co-morbidities such as obesity, type 2 diabetes mellitus, or hyperlipidaemia).

rules.rule_text: Applicants are advised to seek advice in case less stringent criteria for diagnosing NASH-associated cirrhosis are intended to be used.
rules.verbatim_quote: applicants are advised to seek advice in case less stringent criteria are intended to be used.

rules.rule_text: Applicants are advised to submit relevant substantiations/justifications for scientific advice in case a non-invasively diagnosed population is intended to be included in compensated cirrhotic NASH studies.
rules.verbatim_quote: applicants are advised to submit relevant substantiations/justifications for scientific advice in case such a "non-invasively" diagnosed population is intended to be included.

rules.rule_text: In case the proposed endpoint includes a threshold for MELD, an appropriate inclusion criterion for the MELD at baseline will need to be set up that allows measuring relevant deterioration.
rules.verbatim_quote: In case the proposed endpoint includes a threshold for MELD, an appropriate inclusion criterion for the MELD at baseline will need to be set up that allows measuring relevant deterioration.

rules.rule_text: Applicants are advised to ask scientific advice before embarking on a development program in decompensated NASH cirrhosis.
rules.verbatim_quote: applicants are advised to ask scientific advice before embarking on a development program in decompensated NASH cirrhosis.

rules.rule_text: The long-term endpoint in clinical studies for NASH should include a combination of all-cause mortality, liver transplantation, and the manifestation of decompensation (MELD score, variceal bleeding, ascites, encephalopathy etc.).
rules.verbatim_quote: The long-term endpoint in clinical studies for NASH should include a combination of all-cause mortality, liver transplantation, and the manifestation of decompensation (MELD score, variceal bleeding, ascites, encephalopathy etc.).
exceptions: For both cirrhotic and non-cirrhotic NASH, a strategy with the use of intermediate endpoints may apply

rules.rule_text: For non-cirrhotic NASH (fibrosis stage 2 and 3), acceptable intermediate endpoints consist of two composite endpoints to be evaluated at the individual patient level: (1) resolution of NASH with no worsening of fibrosis stage, and (2) improvement of fibrosis by at least 1 stage without any worsening of NASH.
rules.verbatim_quote: Acceptable intermediate endpoints would consist of two composite endpoints to be evaluated at the individual patient level: The resolution of NASH – with the presence of any grade of steatosis, and all of the following: No ballooning, only minimal (grade 1) lobular inflammation and – no worsening of the stage of fibrosis. The improvement of fibrosis by at least 1 stage without any worsening of NASH (no worsening of ballooning and lobular inflammation, not more than 1 grade increase in steatosis).

rules.rule_text: Efficacy for both intermediate endpoints should be demonstrated in co-primary fashion, meaning that both will have to independently demonstrate a statistically significant and clinically relevant difference to placebo.
rules.verbatim_quote: Efficacy for both endpoints should be demonstrated in co-primary fashion, meaning that both will have to independently demonstrate a statistically significant and clinically relevant difference to placebo.

rules.rule_text: Studies in non-cirrhotic NASH will have to be continued at/after interim evaluation (and potential regulatory approval) as long-term studies to document the benefit for clinical endpoints.
rules.verbatim_quote: Studies in non-cirrhotic NASH will have to be continued at/after interim evaluation (and potential regulatory approval) as long-term studies to document the benefit for clinical endpoints.

rules.rule_text: Potential applicants using an intermediate endpoint strategy with conditional approval who cannot meet the two co-primary composite endpoints are advised to seek scientific advice and present their proposal with relevant substantiation based on data.
rules.verbatim_quote: potential applicants are advised to seek scientific advice and present their proposal with relevant substantiation based on data.

rules.rule_text: The long-term outcome for non-cirrhotic NASH is proposed to be a single composite endpoint with any component of the following: all-cause death, decompensation of liver disease, histological diagnosis of progression to liver cirrhosis, and MELD ≥15.
rules.source_section: 5.3.1. Non-cirrhotic NASH (fibrosis stage 2 and 3) – Confirmatory endpoint
rules.verbatim_quote: The long-term outcome for the demonstration of efficacy in non-cirrhotic NASH is therefore proposed to be a single composite endpoint with any component of the following: all-cause death, decompensation of liver disease (with a complete listing), (histological) diagnosis of (progression to) liver cirrhosis, and MELD ≥15.

rules.rule_text: In the absence of approved treatments for NASH, placebo appears to be the only acceptable control treatment for clinical studies, including the long-term extension phases of the studies after evaluation of the intermediate endpoint.
rules.source_section: 5.3.1. Non-cirrhotic NASH (fibrosis stage 2 and 3) – Control group
rules.verbatim_quote: In the absence of approved treatments for NASH, placebo appears to be the only acceptable control treatment for clinical studies. This also applies to the long-term extension phases of the studies after evaluation of the intermediate endpoint.
exceptions: This situation may change when one or more substances have been approved

rules.rule_text: Careful timing of the interim evaluation, filing of the MAA, and conduct of the long-term extension phase is required to protect study integrity.
rules.source_section: 5.3.1. Non-cirrhotic NASH (fibrosis stage 2 and 3) – Control group
rules.verbatim_quote: Careful timing of the interim evaluation, filing of the MAA, and conduct of the long-term extension phase is required.

rules.rule_text: For compensated cirrhotic NASH, the composite endpoint with any of the following events should be used as primary endpoint: decompensation events (variceal bleeding, hepatic encephalopathy, ascites), MELD score at or above 15, liver transplantation, and death.
rules.verbatim_quote: As a general rule, the composite endpoint with any of the following events: decompensation events (variceal bleeding, hepatic encephalopathy, ascites), MELD score at or above 15, liver transplantation, and death should be used as primary endpoint in studies in NASH cirrhosis.
exceptions: A strategy with the use of intermediate endpoints collected at an interim evaluation may also be acceptable in this population

rules.rule_text: Scientific advice is recommended before planning a cirrhotic NASH study without follow-up beyond the intermediate endpoint.
rules.verbatim_quote: Scientific advice is recommended before planning a study without follow-up beyond the intermediate endpoint.

rules.rule_text: In case an intermediate endpoint for reversal of cirrhosis is used in cirrhotic NASH studies, the endpoint must exclude the occurrence of any decompensation event, an increase in MELD, as well as a deterioration of features of NASH activity at the same time.
rules.verbatim_quote: The endpoint would need to exclude the occurrence of any decompensation event, an increase in MELD, as well as as a deterioration (or re-occurrence) of features of NASH activity (inflammation, ballooning, and fat) at the same time (Improvement of cirrhosis by at least one fibrosis grade without occurrence of a decompensation event and without deterioration of MELD and NAS-score).

rules.rule_text: In case an intermediate endpoint is used in cirrhotic NASH, studies should be continued in order to confirm efficacy based on the clinical long-term outcomes like decompensation events, liver transplantation, and death.
rules.verbatim_quote: In case such an intermediate endpoint is used, studies should be continued in order to confirm efficacy based on the clinical long-term outcomes like decompensation events, liver transplantation, and death (=the long-term outcome observation).

rules.rule_text: In case applicants intend to develop treatments for the full spectrum of the disease, it is usually expected that at least one study in each of the sub-populations (cirrhotic and non-cirrhotic NASH) is presented.
rules.source_section: 5.3.2. Cirrhotic NASH – Developments for non-cirrhotic as well as cirrhotic NASH
rules.verbatim_quote: In case applicants intend to develop treatments for the full spectrum of the disease, it is usually expected that at least one study in each of the sub-populations (cirrhotic and non-cirrhotic NASH) is presented.

rules.rule_text: Applicants intending to pursue a development based on the cACLD concept are advised to seek scientific advice before engaging into phase 2 of the clinical development.
rules.source_section: 5.3.2. Cirrhotic NASH – Developments for non-cirrhotic as well as cirrhotic NASH
rules.verbatim_quote: Applicants intending to pursue a development based on this concept are therefore advised to seek scientific advice before engaging into phase 2 of the clinical development.

rules.rule_text: Potential applicants are advised to justify the MELD score proposed based on data in the NASH population, and to have patients undergo a rigorous adjudication for a MELD-related endpoint in order to exclude any non-liver related aetiology.
rules.verbatim_quote: Potential applicants are advised to justify the score proposed based on data in the NASH population, and to have patients undergo a rigorous adjudication for a MELD-related endpoint in order to exclude any non-liver related aetiology (e.g. renal or heart disease).
exceptions: The MELD threshold of 15 is not acceptable in studies in the decompensated population

rules.rule_text: The occurrence of HCC should be evaluated as secondary/exploratory or safety endpoint only, unless the inclusion into the composite can be justified based on data.
rules.source_section: 5.3.3. Additional considerations on endpoints – Hepatocellular carcinoma (HCC)
rules.verbatim_quote: For the time being, the occurrence of HCC should be evaluated as secondary/exploratory or safety (similar to other cancers) endpoint only, unless the inclusion into the composite can be justified based on data.

rules.rule_text: Presence and evaluation of diameter and stigma features of oesophageal varices are not recommended to be part of a primary composite, neither for studies in the non-cirrhotic nor the cirrhotic population.
rules.source_section: 5.3.3. Additional considerations on endpoints – Occurrence/presence of oesophageal varices
rules.verbatim_quote: Presence, and evaluation of diameter and stigma features of oesophageal varices are not recommended to be part of a primary composite, neither for studies the non-cirrhotic (for the clinical endpoint evaluation), nor for the cirrhotic population.

rules.rule_text: It is recommended to include the evaluation of symptoms and health-related quality of life within clinical studies in NASH with adequate and thoroughly validated questionnaires.
rules.source_section: 5.3.3. Additional considerations on endpoints – Patient reported outcomes
rules.verbatim_quote: Nevertheless, it is recommended to include the evaluation of symptoms and health-related quality of life within clinical studies in NASH with adequate and thoroughly validated questionnaires, of which several have been in development in recent years.

rules.rule_text: As a general rule, a two-year interim evaluation is recommended for non-cirrhotic NASH studies, which can be modified based on phase 2 data, the size of the study, patient characteristics, and the requirements with regard to statistical rigor.
rules.source_section: 5.3.3. Additional considerations on endpoints – Duration of studies
rules.verbatim_quote: As a general rule, a two-year interim evaluation is recommended, which can be modified based on phase 2 data, the size of the study, patient characteristics, and the requirements with regard to statistical rigor.
exceptions: The final evaluation, as well as the evaluation in the compensated and decompensated cirrhosis population would be expected to be usually planned with an event-driven evaluation

rules.rule_text: When only one confirmatory study is conducted, potential applicants should be aware of the necessary implications for the need for high quality data with regard to internal and external validity as well as increased statistical rigour (e.g. a two-sided alpha considerably smaller than 5%).
rules.source_section: 5.3.4. Methodological considerations including estimands – One confirmatory study
rules.verbatim_quote: However, potential applicants should be aware on the necessary implications for the need for high quality data with regard to internal and external validity (e.g., consistency across subgroups and endpoints; relevant patient populations evaluated on clinically relevant endpoints) as well as increased statistical rigour (e.g. a two-sided alpha considerably smaller than 5%).

rules.rule_text: The scientific question of interest (the target of estimation/estimand) should be specified in all its attributes and the study planning, design, conduct, analysis and interpretation must be aligned with the estimand.
rules.source_section: 5.3.4. Methodological considerations including estimands – Target of estimation (estimand)
rules.verbatim_quote: The scientific question(s) of interest, i.e. what the study seeks to address and ultimately, the target of estimation (estimand) should be specified in all its attributes. The study planning, design, conduct, analysis and interpretation must be aligned with the estimand.

rules.rule_text: The impact of changes in background treatment and use of rescue medication on the main study endpoints should be evaluated.
rules.source_section: 5.3.4. Methodological considerations including estimands – Target of estimation (estimand)
rules.verbatim_quote: The impact of changes in background treatment and use of rescue medication on the main study endpoints should be evaluated.

rules.rule_text: Choices made regarding design and statistical analysis, including the handling of missing data, must be made considering the target of estimation; sensitivity analyses to support the robustness of the primary analysis should be provided.
rules.source_section: 5.3.4. Methodological considerations including estimands – Design and statistical analysis
rules.verbatim_quote: Choices made regarding design and statistical analysis, including the handling of missing data, must be made considering the target of estimation. [...] Generally, sensitivity analyses to support the robustness of the primary analysis should be provided.

rules.rule_text: For combination treatment development, at least a restricted dose-response exploration of the combination partners and of the combination itself will normally be required.
rules.verbatim_quote: at least a "restricted" dose-response exploration of the combination partners and of the combination itself will normally be required.
exceptions: Any reduction of the exploration of the full dose range will need to be justified

rules.rule_text: For combination treatment development, the contribution to the therapeutic effect is demonstrated for each of the substances involved.
rules.verbatim_quote: The expectations from the regulatory side would be that the combination is based on valid therapeutic principles, but also that for each of the substances involved, the contribution to the therapeutic effect is demonstrated.

rules.rule_text: Biopsies should be undertaken whenever possible for causality assessment of potential drug-induced liver injury in NASH clinical studies.
rules.verbatim_quote: In addition, biopsies should be undertaken whenever possible for causality assessment.

rules.rule_text: The inclusion of experimental biomarkers is highly recommended for studies in patients with underlying liver disease; it is recommended that all these methods are implemented in addition to the routine liver safety evaluation.
rules.verbatim_quote: In addition, the inclusion of experimental biomarkers is highly recommended for studies in patients with underlying liver disease, but the influence of the underlying disease on these markers should be known before they are used to help the assessment of safety. It is recommended that all these methods are implemented in addition to the routine liver safety evaluation.

rules.rule_text: Implementation of (safety) adjudication panels for relevant cardiovascular events is recommended in NASH clinical studies.
rules.verbatim_quote: Implementation of (safety) adjudication panels for relevant cardiovascular events is recommended.

rules.rule_text: Cardiovascular safety documentation will also need to be part of the Risk Management Plan.
rules.verbatim_quote: Cardiovascular safety documentation will also need to be part of the Risk Management Plan.

rules.rule_text: At the point of time it is recommended that relevant clinical studies in paediatric NASH are deferred until sufficient efficacy and safety data in adults are available.
rules.verbatim_quote: At this point of time it is recommended that relevant clinical studies are deferred until sufficient efficacy and safety data in adults are available.
exceptions: Older adolescents may be included into adult studies if adequately justified

rules.rule_text: For paediatric NASH drug development, determination of the appropriate dose (under full consideration of the potential differences in pharmacokinetics in obese and NASH adolescents compared to adults) will be necessary, and the development of age-appropriate formulations as appropriate.
rules.verbatim_quote: Generally, the investigation of the appropriate dose (under full consideration of the potential differences in pharmacokinetics in obese and NASH adolescents compared to adults) will be necessary, and the development of age-appropriate formulations as appropriate.
